ops.title: HF auth on this machine now has write access (OAuth token, verified by real pushes); the old read-only "yasyf-home" token concern is resolved — still preflight the role before writes

ops.body:
The machine is authed as `yasyf` via an **OAuth token** with **write access** — `~/.cache/huggingface/token` starts `hf_oauth`. **2026-07-17: the previous OAuth token went invalid mid-lifetime** ("OAuth token signature verification failed" → 401s even on *public* repos — a bad stored token is worse than none; `HF_HUB_DISABLE_IMPLICIT_TOKEN=1` is the read-path escape hatch). The user re-logged in via `hf auth login --force` (browser device flow) the same day; the new `oauth-yasyf` token is valid (`whoami` → `user=yasyf orgs=anetaco`) and **auto-refreshes on expiry** per the CLI. Whoami shape: `auth == {"type": "oauth", ...}` — **there is NO `auth.accessToken.role` key on an OAuth token.**

Both tokens still coexist in `~/.cache/huggingface/stored_tokens` (the old "gone" claim was wrong): section `[oauth-yasyf]` = the write OAuth token; section `[yasyf-home]` = a classic `hf_…` token whose whoami is `auth.type=access_token`, `accessToken.role=read` — **read-only**, 403s on push. So there is no classic *write* token present; the only write credential is OAuth.

**Why this stays:** the OAuth token expires 2026-08-01 and the failure recurs on re-auth; and the role-preflight shape differs by token type — code that assumes `auth.accessToken.role` KeyErrors on the OAuth token (see [[athome-ensure-write-auth-oauth-keyerror]]).

**How to apply:** to preflight write, branch on `auth["type"]`: `"oauth"` ⇒ treat as write-capable (no role field exists); `"access_token"` ⇒ read `auth.accessToken.role` and require `"write"`. Never index `auth["accessToken"]` unconditionally. If read-only, ask the user to run `! hf auth login` with a write token, or mint a classic write token. Used by cc-pushback auto-sync ([[cc-pushback-two-stage-pipeline]]) and athome's HF push path.
